paper_title: Deep-Learning Control of Lower-Limb Exoskeletons via Simplified Therapist Input

conditions: without external modification of gait features
with external modification of gait features via a user interface

control_system.name: Data-driven controller
control_system.description: Sensor readings are passed to multiple deep-learning models to estimate representative features of the walking pattern. The resulting features are fitted to a normal distribution to consider the uncertainty of the locomotion pattern.

user_interface.purpose: to allow the therapist to modify the self-selected locomotion features and adapt the locomotion depending on the needs of each patient.

results.kinematics: Variation in kinematics according to the gait characteristics.
results.interaction_power: Negative interaction power suggesting exoskeleton assistance across the different conditions.

problem_statement.calibration_time: Lengthy calibration periods for a single session of therapy due to user-dependent control parameters.
problem_statement.limited_customization: Reliance on predefined trajectories for generating assistance profiles limits the degree of parameter customization for individual users.
problem_statement.limited_scenarios: Minimal investigation into scenarios involving stairs and ramps.

proposed_solution.approach: A data-driven controller using deep-learning models to estimate gait features.
proposed_solution.key_innovation: Simplified therapist input via a user interface to modify gait features, reducing the need for extensive parameter calibration.

title: Deep-Learning Control of Lower-Limb Exoskeletons via Simplified Therapist Input
